title: axprobe AX report
description: Machine-readable Agentic Experience (AX) report emitted once per run. This is a stable public interface consumed by regression tooling; field names, types and semantics are a contract.

schema_version.description: Version of this report contract; bumped on breaking changes so consumers can adapt.

scenario.description: Manifest name (the test scenario that was run).

model.description: OpenRouter model id that drove the run. Empty for Layer 0 scripted runs.

outcome.description: Headline terminal state of the run.

goal_reached.description: Whether the manifest goal was actually accomplished — distinct from merely hitting the stop condition or a human gate.

human_interventions.description: Count of gate() calls: how many times a human was required (HIC, the north-star metric).

gate_details.description: For each gate, the 'needs' description: what the human had to provide or decide, and why.

steps.description: Number of driver tool-call rounds taken to reach a terminal state.

commands_run.description: Number of bash() shell commands executed inside the box.

duration_seconds.description: Wall-clock duration of the driver loop, in seconds.

observations.description: Qualitative AX findings recorded by the driver via observe().

false_errors.description: Non-zero exits that did not look like real failures (heuristic-detected): the command reported normal state / a next action.

reason.description: Why it was flagged as a false error.

tokens.description: Token usage and cost, summed across all model calls in the run.

cost_usd.description: Cost in USD as reported by OpenRouter (0 if not reported).

summary.description: Free-text recap from finish(). May be empty when the run ended at a gate (gate() has no summary).
